title: hieracium maculatum subsp. inquinatum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. inquinatum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow flowers and spotted or blotched leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Hieracium maculatum subsp. inquinatum are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using its roots to create a decoction, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
